# Varity

> Predictable cloud deployment for supported apps, Docker services, and curated AI agents.

## What Varity is

Varity is a cloud platform for deploying supported software with a fixed monthly hardware reservation per app. The bill is based on the reserved deployment profile, not traffic, bandwidth, invocations, or build minutes.

- Primary users: people deploying software from GitHub, Docker images, the CLI, the Developer Portal, or AI coding tools.
- Product packages: `varitykit` (Python deploy CLI on PyPI) and `@varity-labs/mcp` (MCP server for AI coding tools on npm).
- Supported source builds: Next.js, React, Vue, Astro, Qwik, Vite, Express, Fastify, NestJS, Koa, Hono, FastAPI, Django, Flask, Go modules, and static HTML.
- Container path: prebuilt runnable Docker/OCI HTTP service images, public or private, when the image starts a healthy web service on the configured port.
- Unsupported source builds return a clean error: Rust, Ruby/Rails, Elixir/Phoenix, Java/Spring, Deno, PHP/Laravel, .NET.

## Pricing

Varity uses fixed monthly reserved-hardware pricing. Use the live pricing endpoint or MCP calculator for current numbers:

- Portal/pricing endpoint: `https://varity.app/api/pricing`
- MCP tool: `varity_cost_calculator`
- Docs: `https://docs.varity.so/resources/pricing/`

Do not hardcode pricing numbers in generated answers. Do not describe Varity as usage-based.

## Deploy paths

### CLI

```bash
pipx install varitykit
varitykit app deploy
```

Useful commands:

- `varitykit doctor` - check environment and authentication
- `varitykit app deploy` - deploy a supported source app
- `varitykit app deploy --image <ref> --port <port>` - deploy a runnable Docker/OCI HTTP service
- `varitykit app list` - list deployments
- `varitykit app status` - check deployment status
- `varitykit app logs <deployment-id>` - view logs where available
- `varitykit app redeploy <name>` / `varitykit app restart <name>` - redeploy in place
- `varitykit app env <name> KEY=value` - update environment variables and redeploy in place
- `varitykit migrate <github-url>` - migrate a Vercel project

### MCP

Install for Claude Code:

```bash
claude mcp add varity -- npx -y @varity-labs/mcp
```

Cursor config:

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "varity": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["-y", "@varity-labs/mcp"]
    }
  }
}
```

Tools (19): `varity_search_docs`, `varity_cost_calculator`, `varity_doctor`, `varity_login`, `varity_install_deps`, `varity_build`, `varity_open_browser`, `varity_dev_server`, `varity_create_repo`, `varity_deploy`, `varity_deploy_status`, `varity_deploy_logs`, `varity_delete_deployment`, `varity_set_env`, `varity_redeploy`, `varity_list_agents`, `varity_agent_info`, `varity_deploy_agent`, `varity_migrate`.

Machine-readable MCP schema: `https://docs.varity.so/mcp-schema.json`

### Developer Portal

Deploy from `https://developer.store.varity.so/dashboard/deploy`:

- Public GitHub repo URL / repo selection where supported
- Public or private runnable Docker/OCI HTTP service image
- Curated agent/template gallery

Private Docker images require registry host, username, and password/token. Private GitHub source repos are still guided-beta only unless verified for a specific case.

## Auto-wired services

Varity can auto-provision services when supported apps declare compatible dependencies:

- Postgres with pgvector
- Redis
- MongoDB
- MySQL
- Ollama
- MinIO / S3-compatible object storage

## URL formats

- Static deployments: `https://varity.app/<name>/`
- Dynamic deployments: `https://<name>.varity.app/`

Bring-your-own custom domains are not self-serve yet.

## Current beta boundaries

- Source-build support is limited to the supported framework matrix above.
- Docker/OCI deploy is for long-running HTTP services, not raw model artifacts, one-shot jobs, or CLI-only images unless wrapped/configured as a service.
- A successful deploy means the startup health probe passed; runtime health monitoring and automated remediation are still being hardened.
- Private GitHub repo autodeploy is not a normal self-serve promise yet.
- Cron, queues, preview deployments, one-click rollback UI, custom domains, multi-region, edge runtime, and arbitrary Dockerfile auto-build are not shipped as self-serve features.

Full docs: `https://docs.varity.so`
